374	/// Errors that can occur during device code generation.
375	#[derive(Debug)]
376	pub enum DeviceCodegenError {
377	    /// No kernels were found to compile.
378	    NoKernels,
379	    /// Failed to enter or exit stable_mir context.
380	    StableMirError(String),
381	    /// MIR to Pliron IR translation failed.
382	    Translation(String),
383	    /// PTX generation (llc invocation) failed.
384	    PtxGeneration(String),
385	    /// A `#[device] extern` signature could not be represented exactly.
386	    InvalidDeviceExternSignature(String),
387	    /// IO error (file read/write).
388	    Io(std::io::Error),
389	}
390	
391	impl std::fmt::Display for DeviceCodegenError {
392	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
393	        match self {
394	            Self::NoKernels => write!(f, "No kernel functions found"),
395	            Self::StableMirError(msg) => write!(f, "stable_mir error: {}", msg),
396	            Self::Translation(msg) => write!(f, "Translation failed: {}", msg),
397	            Self::PtxGeneration(msg) => write!(f, "PTX generation failed: {}", msg),
398	            Self::InvalidDeviceExternSignature(msg) => {
399	                write!(f, "Invalid device-extern signature: {msg}")
400	            }
401	            Self::Io(e) => write!(f, "IO error: {}", e),
402	        }
403	    }
404	}
405	
406	impl std::error::Error for DeviceCodegenError {}
407	
408	impl From<std::io::Error> for DeviceCodegenError {
409	    fn from(e: std::io::Error) -> Self {
410	        Self::Io(e)
411	    }
412	}
413	
414	/// Generates PTX for device functions using the cuda-oxide pipeline.
415	///
416	/// This is the main entry point for device codegen. It bridges between
417	/// rustc's internal types (`rustc_middle`) and mir-importer's stable_mir-based
418	/// pipeline (`rustc_public`).
419	///
420	/// ## Parameters
421	///
422	/// - `tcx`: The type context from rustc
423	/// - `functions`: Collected device functions from the collector module
424	/// - `config`: Output and diagnostic configuration
425	///
426	/// ## Returns
427	///
428	/// - `Ok(DeviceCodegenResult)`: Paths to generated .ll and .ptx files
429	/// - `Err(DeviceCodegenError)`: Description of what went wrong
430	///
431	/// ## Pipeline Stages
432	///
433	/// ```text
434	/// CollectedFunction<'tcx>
435	///         │
436	///         ├──▶ rustc_internal::stable() ──▶ rustc_public::Instance
437	///         │
438	///         └──▶ mir_importer::run_pipeline()
439	///                     │
440	///                     ├──▶ `dialect-mir` (alloca form)
441	///                     │
442	///                     ├──▶ `dialect-mir` (mem2reg → SSA)
443	///                     ├──▶ annotated loop unroll
444	///                     │
445	///                     ├──▶ LLVM dialect
446	///                     │
447	///                     ├──▶ textual LLVM IR (.ll)
448	///                     │
449	///                     └──▶ PTX (.ptx) via `llc`
450	/// ```
451	pub fn generate_device_code<'tcx>(
452	    tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,
453	    functions: &[CollectedFunction<'tcx>],
454	    device_externs: &[DeviceExternDecl],
455	    config: &DeviceCodegenConfig,
456	) -> Result<DeviceCodegenResult, DeviceCodegenError> {
457	    use rustc_public::rustc_internal;
458	
459	    if functions.is_empty() {
460	        return Err(DeviceCodegenError::NoKernels);
461	    }
462	
463	    if config.verbose {
464	        eprintln!(
465	            "[device_codegen] Compiling {} functions, {} device externs to PTX via cuda-oxide pipeline",
466	            functions.len(),
467	            device_externs.len()
468	        );
469	        for func in functions {
470	            eprintln!(
471	                "[device_codegen]   {} {}",
472	                if func.is_kernel { "kernel" } else { "device" },
473	                func.export_name
474	            );
475	        }
476	        for decl in device_externs {
477	            eprintln!(
478	                "[device_codegen]   extern {} (convergent={}, pure={}, readonly={})",
479	                decl.export_name,
480	                decl.attrs.is_convergent,
481	                decl.attrs.is_pure,
482	                decl.attrs.is_readonly
483	            );
484	        }
485	    }
486	
487	    // Prepare data we need to pass into the stable_mir closure
488	    // (closures can't capture references to local TyCtxt data)
489	    let export_names: Vec<(String, bool)> = functions
490	        .iter()
491	        .map(|f| (f.export_name.clone(), f.is_kernel))
492	        .collect();
493	    let debug_scope_maps: Vec<_> = functions
494	        .iter()
495	        .map(|f| build_debug_source_scope_map(tcx, f))
496	        .collect();
497	
498	    // Convert device externs to mir-importer format
499	    // We extract signature info from rustc here since we have access to TyCtxt
500	    let stable_device_externs: Vec<mir_importer::DeviceExternDecl> = device_externs
501	        .iter()
502	        .map(|decl| {
503	            // Get function signature from rustc
504	            let fn_sig = tcx.fn_sig(decl.def_id).instantiate_identity();
505	            let fn_sig = fn_sig.skip_binder();
506	
507	            if !matches!(fn_sig.abi, rustc_abi::ExternAbi::C { unwind: false }) {
508	                return Err(DeviceCodegenError::InvalidDeviceExternSignature(format!(
509	                    "`{}` uses ABI {:?}; device externs must use `extern \"C\"` without unwinding",
510	                    decl.export_name, fn_sig.abi
511	                )));
512	            }
513	
514	            if fn_sig.c_variadic {
515	                return Err(DeviceCodegenError::InvalidDeviceExternSignature(format!(
516	                    "`{}` is variadic; variadic device externs are not supported",
517	                    decl.export_name
518	                )));
519	            }
520	
521	            let param_types = fn_sig
522	                .inputs()
523	                .iter()
524	                .enumerate()
525	                .map(|(index, ty)| {
526	                    rustc_ty_to_device_extern_type(tcx, *ty, DeviceExternTypePosition::Parameter)
527	                        .map_err(|reason| {
528	                            DeviceCodegenError::InvalidDeviceExternSignature(format!(
529	                                "`{}` parameter {} (`{ty}`): {reason}",
530	                                decl.export_name, index
531	                            ))
532	                        })
533	                })
534	                .collect::<Result<Vec<_>, _>>()?;
535	
536	            let result_ty = fn_sig.output();
537	            let return_type =
538	                rustc_ty_to_device_extern_type(tcx, result_ty, DeviceExternTypePosition::Result)
539	                    .map_err(|reason| {
540	                        DeviceCodegenError::InvalidDeviceExternSignature(format!(
541	                            "`{}` result (`{result_ty}`): {reason}",
542	                            decl.export_name
543	                        ))
544	                    })?;
545	
546	            Ok(mir_importer::DeviceExternDecl {
547	                export_name: decl.export_name.clone(),
548	                param_types,
549	                return_type,
550	                attrs: mir_importer::DeviceExternAttrs {
551	                    is_convergent: decl.attrs.is_convergent,
552	                    is_pure: decl.attrs.is_pure,
553	                    is_readonly: decl.attrs.is_readonly,
554	                },
555	            })
556	        })
557	        .collect::<Result<_, _>>()?;
558	
559	    let output_dir = config.output_dir.clone();
560	    let output_name = config.output_name.clone();
561	    let verbose = config.verbose;
562	    let show_rustc_mir = config.dump_rustc_mir;
563	    let show_mir = config.dump_mir_dialect;
564	    let show_llvm = config.dump_llvm_dialect;
565	
566	    // Print raw rustc MIR if requested (before conversion to stable_mir)
567	    if show_rustc_mir {
568	        use rustc_middle::ty::print::with_no_trimmed_paths;
569	
570	        eprintln!();
571	        eprintln!("=== Rustc MIR (before translation) ===");
572	        for func in functions {
573	            let mir = tcx.instance_mir(func.instance.def);
574	            eprintln!();
575	            eprintln!("fn {} {{", func.export_name);
576	
577	            // Print locals
578	            eprintln!(
579	                "    let mut _0: {:?};",
580	                mir.local_decls[rustc_middle::mir::Local::from_u32(0)].ty
581	            );
582	            for (local, decl) in mir.local_decls.iter_enumerated().skip(1) {
583	                let mutability = if decl.mutability == rustc_middle::mir::Mutability::Mut {
584	                    "mut "
585	                } else {
586	                    ""
587	                };
588	                eprintln!("    let {}_{}:  {:?};", mutability, local.index(), decl.ty);
589	            }
590	
591	            // Print debug info
592	            for debug_info in &mir.var_debug_info {
593	                with_no_trimmed_paths!(eprintln!(
594	                    "    debug {:?} => {:?};",
595	                    debug_info.name, debug_info.value
596	                ));
597	            }
598	
599	            // Print basic blocks
600	            for (bb_idx, bb_data) in mir.basic_blocks.iter_enumerated() {
601	                eprintln!("    bb{}: {{", bb_idx.index());
602	                for stmt in &bb_data.statements {
603	                    with_no_trimmed_paths!(eprintln!("        {:?}", stmt));
604	                }
605	                with_no_trimmed_paths!(eprintln!("        {:?}", bb_data.terminator().kind));
606	                eprintln!("    }}");
607	            }
608	            eprintln!("}}");
609	        }
610	        eprintln!();
611	    }
612	
613	    // Enter stable_mir context and run the pipeline.
614	    //
615	    // rustc_internal::run() does the following:
616	    // 1. Creates Tables for type/instance interning
617	    // 2. Sets up thread-local CompilerCtxt
618	    // 3. Runs our closure with access to stable() conversion
619	    // 4. Tears down the context and returns our result
620	    // Pre-compute `#[inline(always)]` flags before entering the stable_mir
621	    // context, since the query lives on `rustc_middle::TyCtxt` and is not
622	    // exposed through stable_mir. Preserving this hint avoids making helper
623	    // boundaries depend entirely on later optimizer heuristics.
624	    let inline_always_flags: Vec<bool> = functions
625	        .iter()
626	        .map(|func| {
627	            let def_id = func.instance.def_id();
628	            matches!(
629	                tcx.codegen_fn_attrs(def_id).inline,
630	                rustc_hir::attrs::InlineAttr::Always | rustc_hir::attrs::InlineAttr::Force { .. }
631	            )
632	        })
633	        .collect();
634	
635	    let result = rustc_internal::run(tcx, || {
636	        // Convert internal Instance<'tcx> to stable_mir Instance
637	        let stable_functions: Vec<mir_importer::CollectedFunction> = functions
638	            .iter()
639	            .zip(export_names.iter())
640	            .zip(debug_scope_maps.iter())
641	            .zip(inline_always_flags.iter())
642	            .map(
643	                |(((func, (export_name, is_kernel)), debug_source_scopes), is_inline_always)| {
644	                    // Use rustc_internal::stable() to convert the Instance.
645	                    // This is the key bridge between rustc_middle and rustc_public types.
646	                    let stable_instance = rustc_internal::stable(func.instance);
647	
648	                    mir_importer::CollectedFunction {
649	                        instance: stable_instance,
650	                        is_kernel: *is_kernel,
651	                        export_name: export_name.clone(),
652	                        debug_source_scopes: Some(debug_source_scopes.clone()),
653	                        is_inline_always: *is_inline_always,
654	                    }
655	                },
656	            )
657	            .collect();
658	
659	        // Check for NVVM IR mode (set by cargo oxide --emit-nvvm-ir)
660	        let emit_nvvm_ir = std::env::var("CUDA_OXIDE_EMIT_NVVM_IR").is_ok();
661	
662	        if verbose {
663	            eprintln!(
664	                "[device_codegen] Converted {} functions to stable_mir format",
665	                stable_functions.len()
666	            );
667	            if emit_nvvm_ir {
668	                eprintln!("[device_codegen] NVVM IR mode enabled");
669	            }
670	        }
671	
672	        let debug_kind = device_debug_kind(tcx.sess.opts.debuginfo);
673	        let target_arch = std::env::var("CUDA_OXIDE_TARGET").ok();
674	        let device_arch_hint = std::env::var("CUDA_OXIDE_DEVICE_ARCH").ok();
675	        let allow_fma_contraction = std::env::var_os("CUDA_OXIDE_NO_FMA").is_none();
676	
677	        if verbose && !allow_fma_contraction {
678	            eprintln!("[device_codegen] FMA contraction disabled");
679	        }
680	
681	        // Create pipeline config
682	        let pipeline_config = mir_importer::PipelineConfig {
683	            output_dir: output_dir.clone(),
684	            output_name: output_name.clone(),
685	            verbose,
686	            show_mir_dialect: show_mir,
687	            show_llvm_dialect: show_llvm,
688	            emit_nvvm_ir,
689	            target_arch,
690	            device_arch_hint,
691	            debug_kind,
692	            allow_fma_contraction,
693	        };
694	
695	        // Run the cuda-oxide pipeline!
696	        // Rust MIR → `dialect-mir` → mem2reg → unroll → LLVM dialect → LLVM IR → PTX.
697	        // Device externs are emitted as `declare` statements in LLVM IR
698	        mir_importer::run_pipeline(&stable_functions, &stable_device_externs, &pipeline_config)
699	    });
700	
701	    // Handle the result from rustc_internal::run.
702	    // We have nested Results: outer from run(), inner from run_pipeline().
703	    match result {
704	        Ok(pipeline_result) => match pipeline_result {
705	            Ok(compilation_result) => {
706	                let artifact = read_compilation_artifact(&compilation_result)?;
707	                let ptx_content = match artifact.as_ref() {
708	                    Some(artifact) if artifact.kind == DeviceCodegenArtifactKind::Ptx => {
709	                        Some(String::from_utf8(artifact.bytes.clone()).map_err(|e| {
710	                            DeviceCodegenError::PtxGeneration(format!(
711	                                "generated PTX is not valid UTF-8: {e}"
712	                            ))
713	                        })?)
714	                    }
715	                    _ => None,
716	                };
717	
718	                if config.verbose {
719	                    if let Some(artifact) = artifact.as_ref() {
720	                        eprintln!(
721	                            "[device_codegen] Embeddable artifact generated: {} ({:?}, target: {})",
722	                            artifact.name, artifact.kind, compilation_result.target
723	                        );
724	                    } else {
725	                        eprintln!(
726	                            "[device_codegen] No embeddable artifact found for {} (target: {})",
727	                            compilation_result.ll_path.display(),
728	                            compilation_result.target
729	                        );
730	                    }
731	                }
732	
733	                Ok(DeviceCodegenResult {
734	                    ptx_path: compilation_result.ptx_path,
735	                    ll_path: compilation_result.ll_path,
736	                    target: compilation_result.target,
737	                    ptx_content,
738	                    artifact,
739	                    allow_fma_contraction: compilation_result.allow_fma_contraction,
740	                })
741	            }
742	            Err(pipeline_err) => Err(DeviceCodegenError::PtxGeneration(format!(
743	                "{}",
744	                pipeline_err
745	            ))),
746	        },
747	        Err(stable_mir_err) => Err(DeviceCodegenError::StableMirError(format!(
748	            "{:?}",
749	            stable_mir_err
750	        ))),
751	    }
752	}
753	
754	fn device_debug_kind(rustc_debug: DebugInfo) -> llvm_export::export::DebugKind {
755	    device_debug_kind_with_override(
756	        rustc_debug,
757	        std::env::var("CUDA_OXIDE_DEBUG").ok().as_deref(),
758	    )
759	}
760	
761	fn device_debug_kind_with_override(
762	    rustc_debug: DebugInfo,
763	    override_value: Option<&str>,
764	) -> llvm_export::export::DebugKind {
765	    if let Some(value) = override_value {
766	        match value.trim().to_ascii_lowercase().as_str() {
767	            "0" | "off" | "none" => return llvm_export::export::DebugKind::Off,
768	            "1" | "line" | "lines" | "line-tables" | "line-tables-only" => {
769	                return llvm_export::export::DebugKind::LineTables;
770	            }
771	            "2" | "full" => return llvm_export::export::DebugKind::Full,
772	            _ => {}
773	        }
774	    }
775	
776	    match rustc_debug {
777	        DebugInfo::None => llvm_export::export::DebugKind::Off,
778	        DebugInfo::LineDirectivesOnly
779	        | DebugInfo::LineTablesOnly
780	        | DebugInfo::Limited
781	        | DebugInfo::Full => llvm_export::export::DebugKind::LineTables,
782	    }
783	}
784	
785	fn read_compilation_artifact(
786	    result: &mir_importer::CompilationResult,
787	) -> Result<Option<DeviceCodegenArtifact>, DeviceCodegenError> {
788	    let kind = match result.artifact_kind {
789	        mir_importer::CompilationArtifactKind::Ptx => DeviceCodegenArtifactKind::Ptx,
790	        mir_importer::CompilationArtifactKind::NvvmIr => DeviceCodegenArtifactKind::NvvmIr,
791	        mir_importer::CompilationArtifactKind::Ltoir => DeviceCodegenArtifactKind::Ltoir,
792	        mir_importer::CompilationArtifactKind::Cubin => DeviceCodegenArtifactKind::Cubin,
793	    };
794	
795	    match std::fs::read(&result.artifact_path) {
796	        Ok(bytes) => Ok(Some(DeviceCodegenArtifact {
797	            kind,
798	            name: result
799	                .artifact_path
800	                .file_name()
801	                .and_then(|name| name.to_str())
802	                .unwrap_or("device-artifact")
803	                .to_string(),
804	            bytes,
805	        })),
806	        Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => Ok(None),
807	        Err(e) => Err(DeviceCodegenError::Io(e)),
808	    }
809	}
810	
811	#[cfg(test)]
812	mod tests {
813	    use super::*;
814	
815	    #[test]
816	    fn test_config_default() {
817	        let config = DeviceCodegenConfig::default();
818	        assert!(!config.verbose);
819	        assert_eq!(config.output_name, "kernel");
820	    }
821	
822	    #[test]
823	    fn device_debug_kind_follows_rustc_debuginfo() {
824	        assert_eq!(
825	            device_debug_kind_with_override(DebugInfo::None, None),
826	            llvm_export::export::DebugKind::Off
827	        );
828	        assert_eq!(
829	            device_debug_kind_with_override(DebugInfo::LineTablesOnly, None),
830	            llvm_export::export::DebugKind::LineTables
831	        );
832	        assert_eq!(
833	            device_debug_kind_with_override(DebugInfo::Full, None),
834	            llvm_export::export::DebugKind::LineTables
835	        );
836	    }
837	
838	    #[test]
839	    fn device_debug_kind_env_override_wins() {
840	        assert_eq!(
841	            device_debug_kind_with_override(DebugInfo::Full, Some("off")),
842	            llvm_export::export::DebugKind::Off
843	        );
844	        assert_eq!(
845	            device_debug_kind_with_override(DebugInfo::None, Some(" Line-Tables ")),
846	            llvm_export::export::DebugKind::LineTables
847	        );
848	        assert_eq!(
849	            device_debug_kind_with_override(DebugInfo::None, Some("full")),
850	            llvm_export::export::DebugKind::Full
851	        );
852	    }
853	
854	    #[test]
855	    fn read_compilation_artifact_uses_declared_nvvm_ir_path() {
856	        let temp_dir = unique_temp_dir("cuda-codegen-artifact");
857	        std::fs::create_dir_all(&temp_dir).unwrap();
858	        let ll_path = temp_dir.join("demo.ll");
859	        let ptx_path = temp_dir.join("demo.ptx");
860	        std::fs::write(&ll_path, b"nvvm ir").unwrap();
861	        std::fs::write(&ptx_path, b"stale ptx").unwrap();
862	
863	        let result = mir_importer::CompilationResult {
864	            ll_path: ll_path.clone(),
865	            ptx_path,
866	            artifact_path: ll_path,
867	            artifact_kind: mir_importer::CompilationArtifactKind::NvvmIr,
868	            target: "sm_90".to_string(),
869	            allow_fma_contraction: false,
870	        };
871	
872	        let artifact = read_compilation_artifact(&result).unwrap().unwrap();
873	        assert_eq!(artifact.kind, DeviceCodegenArtifactKind::NvvmIr);
874	        assert_eq!(artifact.name, "demo.ll");
875	        assert_eq!(artifact.bytes, b"nvvm ir");
876	
877	        let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(temp_dir);
878	    }
879	
880	    #[test]
881	    fn read_compilation_artifact_reads_declared_cubin_path() {
882	        let temp_dir = unique_temp_dir("cuda-codegen-artifact");
883	        std::fs::create_dir_all(&temp_dir).unwrap();
884	        let ll_path = temp_dir.join("demo.ll");
885	        let cubin_path = temp_dir.join("demo.cubin");
886	        let ptx_path = temp_dir.join("demo.ptx");
887	        std::fs::write(&cubin_path, b"cubin").unwrap();
888	
889	        let result = mir_importer::CompilationResult {
890	            ll_path,
891	            ptx_path,
892	            artifact_path: cubin_path,
893	            artifact_kind: mir_importer::CompilationArtifactKind::Cubin,
894	            target: "sm_90".to_string(),
895	            allow_fma_contraction: true,
896	        };
897	
898	        let artifact = read_compilation_artifact(&result).unwrap().unwrap();
899	        assert_eq!(artifact.kind, DeviceCodegenArtifactKind::Cubin);
900	        assert_eq!(artifact.name, "demo.cubin");
901	        assert_eq!(artifact.bytes, b"cubin");
902	
903	        let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(temp_dir);
904	    }
905	
906	    fn unique_temp_dir(name: &str) -> PathBuf {
907	        let nanos = std::time::SystemTime::now()
908	            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
909	            .unwrap()
910	            .as_nanos();
911	        std::env::temp_dir().join(format!("{name}-{}-{nanos}", std::process::id()))
912	    }
913	}

1	/*
2	 * SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright (c) 2026 NVIDIA CORPORATION & AFFILIATES. All rights reserved.

5	
6	//! # rustc_codegen_cuda: Unified Host/Device Compilation Backend
7	//!
8	//! A custom rustc codegen backend that enables single-source CUDA compilation for Rust,
9	//! similar to NVIDIA's nvc++ compiler for C++. This backend intercepts rustc's code
10	//! generation phase to extract device code and compile it to PTX while delegating
11	//! host code compilation to the standard LLVM backend.
12	//!
13	//! ## Overview
14	//!
15	//! ```text
16	//! ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
17	//! │                              RUSTC COMPILATION                                  │
18	//! │                                                                                 │
19	//! │   Source Code (.rs)                                                             │
20	//! │         │                                                                       │
21	//! │         ▼                                                                       │
22	//! │   ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐     │
23	//! │   │                         RUSTC FRONTEND                                │     │
24	//! │   │                                                                       │     │
25	//! │   │   Parsing ──▶ HIR ──▶ Type Check ──▶ MIR Generation ──▶ MIR Passes    │     │
26	//! │   │                                                                       │     │
27	//! │   │   Outputs: Fully monomorphized, OPTIMIZED MIR                         │     │
28	//! │   │            (affected by -C opt-level, -Z mir-enable-passes)           │     │
29	//! │   └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘     │
30	//! │         │                                                                       │
31	//! │         │  MIR passes have ALREADY run by this point                            │
32	//! │         │  (including JumpThreading unless disabled)                            │
33	//! │         ▼                                                                       │
34	//! │   ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐     │
35	//! │   │                    rustc_codegen_cuda (THIS BACKEND)                  │     │
36	//! │   │                                                                       │     │
37	//! │   │   Entry: codegen_crate(TyCtxt) called by rustc                        │     │
38	//! │   │                                                                       │     │
39	//! │   │   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐     │     │
40	//! │   │   │  1. KERNEL DETECTION                                        │     │     │
41	//! │   │   │     - Scan CGUs for functions in the reserved namespace     │     │     │
42	//! │   │   │       `cuda_oxide_kernel_<hash>_*` (set by #[kernel] macro) │     │     │
43	//! │   │   └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘     │     │
44	//! │   │                          │                                            │     │
45	//! │   │                          ▼                                            │     │
46	//! │   │   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐     │     │
47	//! │   │   │  2. DEVICE FUNCTION COLLECTION (collector.rs)               │     │     │
48	//! │   │   │     - Start from kernel entry points                        │     │     │
49	//! │   │   │     - Walk MIR call graph transitively                      │     │     │
50	//! │   │   │     - Collect all reachable functions from:                 │     │     │
51	//! │   │   │       • Local crate                                         │     │     │
52	//! │   │   │       • cuda_device (intrinsics)                            │     │     │
53	//! │   │   │       • core (iterators, Option, etc.)                      │     │     │
54	//! │   │   │     - Filter out: fmt::*, panicking::*, intrinsic stubs     │     │     │
55	//! │   │   └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘     │     │
56	//! │   │                          │                                            │     │
57	//! │   │          ┌───────────────┴───────────────┐                            │     │
58	//! │   │          ▼                               ▼                            │     │
59	//! │   │   ┌─────────────────┐           ┌─────────────────────────┐           │     │
60	//! │   │   │  DEVICE PATH    │           │      HOST PATH          │           │     │
61	//! │   │   │                 │           │                         │           │     │
62	//! │   │   │  3. Bridge to   │           │  4. Delegate to         │           │     │
63	//! │   │   │     stable_mir  │           │     rustc_codegen_llvm  │           │     │
64	//! │   │   │                 │           │                         │           │     │
65	//! │   │   │  device_codegen │           │  Standard LLVM backend  │           │     │
66	//! │   │   │  .rs handles    │           │  handles all host code  │           │     │
67	//! │   │   └────────┬────────┘           └────────────┬────────────┘           │     │
68	//! │   │            │                                 │                        │     │
69	//! │   │            ▼                                 ▼                        │     │
70	//! │   │   ┌─────────────────┐           ┌─────────────────────────┐           │     │
71	//! │   │   │ cuda-oxide      │           │  Host Object Files      │           │     │
72	//! │   │   │ Pipeline:       │           │  (.o / .rlib)           │           │     │
73	//! │   │   │                 │           │                         │           │     │
74	//! │   │   │ dialect-mir     │           │  Standard x86_64 code   │           │     │
75	//! │   │   │     ▼ (mem2reg) │           │                         │           │     │
76	//! │   │   │ LLVM dialect    │           │                         │           │     │
77	//! │   │   │     ▼           │           │                         │           │     │
78	//! │   │   │ LLVM IR (.ll)   │           │                         │           │     │
79	//! │   │   │     ▼ (llc)     │           │                         │           │     │
80	//! │   │   │ PTX (.ptx)      │           │                         │           │     │
81	//! │   │   └─────────────────┘           └─────────────────────────┘           │     │
82	//! │   │                                                                       │     │
83	//! │   └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘     │
84	//! │                                                                                 │
85	//! └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
86	//! ```
87	//!
88	//! ## How MIR is Obtained
89	//!
90	//! When `codegen_crate()` is called, rustc has ALREADY:
91	//!
92	//! 1. **Parsed** the source code
93	//! 2. **Type checked** everything
94	//! 3. **Generated MIR** for all functions
95	//! 4. **Run MIR optimization passes** based on `-C opt-level` and `-Z mir-enable-passes`
96	//!
97	//! We receive a `TyCtxt` containing **optimized MIR**. The MIR we get depends entirely
98	//! on what flags were passed to rustc:
99	//!
100	//! ```text
101	//! ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
102	//! │                           MIR OPTIMIZATION PASSES                               │
103	//! │                                                                                 │
104	//! │   User runs:  rustc -C opt-level=3 -Z mir-enable-passes=-JumpThreading ...      │
105	//! │                         │                        │                              │
106	//! │                         ▼                        ▼                              │
107	//! │              ┌──────────────────┐    ┌──────────────────────────┐               │
108	//! │              │ Enable passes:   │    │ Disable passes:          │               │
109	//! │              │ - Inlining       │    │ - JumpThreading (MUST!)  │               │
110	//! │              │ - ConstProp      │    │                          │               │
111	//! │              │ - GVN            │    │                          │               │
112	//! │              │ - DeadCode       │    │                          │               │
113	//! │              │ - etc.           │    │                          │               │
114	//! │              └──────────────────┘    └──────────────────────────┘               │
115	//! │                                                                                 │
116	//! │   Result: We get MIR that has been through these passes                         │
117	//! │           This affects BOTH host and device code (same MIR for both)            │
118	//! │                                                                                 │
119	//! └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
120	//! ```
121	//!
122	//! ## Required Compiler Flags
123	//!
124	//! The following flags MUST be passed to rustc when using this backend:
125	//!
126	//! | Flag                                  | Purpose                | Why Required                                                                                               |
127	//! |---------------------------------------|------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
128	//! | `-Z mir-enable-passes=-JumpThreading` | Disable JumpThreading  | **CRITICAL**: JumpThreading duplicates barrier calls into branches, breaking GPU synchronization semantics |
129	//!
130	//! Recommended for production:
131	//!
132	//! | Flag                       | Purpose                  | Why Recommended                                              |
133	//! |----------------------------|--------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
134	//! | `-C opt-level=3`           | Maximum MIR optimization | Better inlining, smaller device code                         |
135	//! | `-C debug-assertions=off`  | Remove debug checks      | `debug_assert!` pulls in fmt code that can't compile for GPU |
136	//!
137	//! **Note:** `panic=abort` is **NOT required**. The codegen backend treats all unwind
138	//! paths as unreachable since the CUDA toolchain does not support unwinding today. This means standard library code
139	//! compiled without `panic=abort` works fine -- unwind edges are simply ignored.
140	//!
141	//! ### Why JumpThreading Must Be Disabled
142	//!
143	//! JumpThreading is a MIR optimization that duplicates code to eliminate jumps.
144	//! This is problematic for GPU code because it can duplicate barrier calls:
145	//!
146	//! ```text
147	//! BEFORE JumpThreading:              AFTER JumpThreading (BROKEN!):
148	//! ┌─────────────────────────┐        ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
149	//! │ bb0:                    │        │ bb0:                                │
150	//! │   if cond -> bb1, bb2   │        │   if cond -> bb1, bb2               │
151	//! │                         │        │                                     │
152	//! │ bb1:                    │        │ bb1:                                │
153	//! │   a()                   │        │   a()                               │
154	//! │   goto bb3              │        │   __syncthreads()  ◄─── Thread 0-15 │
155	//! │                         │        │   c()                               │
156	//! │ bb2:                    │        │   return                            │
157	//! │   goto bb3              │        │                                     │
158	//! │                         │        │ bb2:                                │
159	//! │ bb3:                    │        │   __syncthreads()  ◄─── Thread 16-31│
160	//! │   __syncthreads()       │        │   c()                               │
161	//! │   c()                   │        │   return                            │
162	//! │   return                │        │                                     │
163	//! └─────────────────────────┘        └─────────────────────────────────────┘
164	//!
165	//! Different threads execute DIFFERENT barrier instances = DEADLOCK!
166	//! ```
167	//!
168	//! ## `no_std` Requirement
169	//!
170	//! Kernel crates MUST use `#![no_std]`. The collector enforces this with a
171	//! single hard rule: **the `std` crate itself is forbidden, every other
172	//! crate is allowed** (provided it's reachable from a kernel and itself
173	//! avoids `std`). The check is on the *originating crate*
174	//! (`tcx.crate_name(def_id.krate)`), not on display paths -- which matters,
175	//! because rustc's MIR pretty-printer routinely emits `std::*` for items
176	//! that are merely re-exported from `core`.
177	//!
178	//! See [`collector::should_collect_from_crate`] for the exact policy.
179	//!
180	//! ### Why `std::*` shows up in MIR dumps (and isn't a problem)
181	//!
182	//! Run `cargo oxide pipeline vecadd` (or `atomics`, or most other examples)
183	//! and the rustc MIR section will be peppered with paths like:
184	//!
185	//! ```text
186	//! _4 = std::option::Option::<&mut f32>::Some(copy _21)
187	//! _4 = const std::option::Option::<&mut f32>::None
188	//! _3 = copy _14 as *const std::sync::atomic::Atomic<u32> (PtrToPtr)
189	//! _4 = std::intrinsics::atomic_xadd::<u32, u32, ...>(move _15, ...) -> ...
190	//! ```
191	//!
192	//! These are **`core` items shown under their `std::` re-export path**.
193	//! `def_path_str` chooses the most user-visible path, which is usually the
194	//! `std::*` form. The actual `DefId` lives in `core` (or `core::sync::atomic`,
195	//! `core::intrinsics`, ...), so the collector's
196	//! `crate_name(def_id.krate) == "std"` check is `false` and they're collected
197	//! normally. Treat `std::*` in MIR output as cosmetic; only a hard collector
198	//! error means actual `std` was reached.
199	//!
200	//! ```text
201	//! ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
202	//! │                        CRATE FILTERING FOR DEVICE CODE                          │
203	//! │                                                                                 │
204	//! │   Allowed (originating crate, i.e. DefId.krate):                                │
205	//! │   ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐      │
206	//! │   │ local crate (your kernel code)                                       │      │
207	//! │   │ cuda_device  (GPU intrinsics)                                        │      │
208	//! │   │ core         (Option, Result, UnsafeCell, sync::atomic, intrinsics)  │      │
209	//! │   │ alloc        (Vec / Box, only if you wired up a GPU allocator)       │      │
210	//! │   │ any other no_std crate, if transitively reachable from a kernel      │      │
211	//! │   │   (libm, num-traits, your own helper crates, ...)                    │      │
212	//! │   └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘      │
213	//! │                                                                                 │
214	//! │   Forbidden (hard error at collection time):                                    │
215	//! │   ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐      │
216	//! │   │ std -- only when the *originating* crate is std (not just a display  │      │
217	//! │   │        re-export). Example: an actual call into std::thread,         │      │
218	//! │   │        std::fs, std::io, std::sync::Mutex, etc.                      │      │
219	//! │   └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘      │
220	//! │                                                                                 │
221	//! │   When that genuine std call is reached, the collector emits a                  │
222	//! │   CollectDecision::Forbidden, and process_call_operand aborts compilation       │
223	//! │   with a formatted error box naming the function -- no silent skip, no          │
224	//! │   cryptic PTX "undefined symbol" later in the pipeline.                         │
225	//! │                                                                                 │
226	//! │   Intentionally skipped (no error, just dropped): `core::fmt::*`,               │
227	//! │   `core::panicking::*`, and `*::precondition_check`. These are reached          │
228	//! │   by panic/UB-check paths that can't actually fire at runtime under             │
229	//! │   panic=abort + `-C debug-assertions=off`.                                      │
230	//! │                                                                                 │
231	//! └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
232	//! ```
233	//!
234	//! ## Compilation Model
235	//!
236	//! **Unified single-source** compilation is fully supported. Device code is marked
237	//! with `#[kernel]` and the backend automatically splits based on kernel reachability
238	//! -- no `#[cfg(cuda_device)]` needed.
239	//!
240	//! ```rust,ignore
241	//! use cuda_device::{kernel, thread, DisjointSlice};
242	//! use cuda_host::cuda_launch;
243	//!
244	//! #[kernel]
245	//! pub fn vecadd(a: &[f32], b: &[f32], mut c: DisjointSlice<f32>) {
246	//!     let idx = thread::index_1d();
247	//!     if let Some(c_elem) = c.get_mut(idx) {
248	//!         *c_elem = a[idx.get()] + b[idx.get()];
249	//!     }
250	//! }
251	//!
252	//! fn main() {
253	//!     // Host code -- compiled to native x86_64 by LLVM
254	//!     // Kernel is compiled to PTX by cuda-oxide
255	//! }
256	//! ```
257	//!
258	//! See `examples/` for working examples.
259	//!
260	//! ## Example Usage
261	//!
262	//! ```bash
263	//! # Build the backend
264	//! cd crates/rustc-codegen-cuda
265	//! cargo build
266	//!
267	//! # Compile a kernel crate with the backend
268	//! CUDA_OXIDE_VERBOSE=1 rustc \
269	//!     --edition 2021 \
270	//!     -C opt-level=3 \
271	//!     -C debug-assertions=off \
272	//!     -Z mir-enable-passes=-JumpThreading \
273	//!     -Z codegen-backend=./target/debug/librustc_codegen_cuda.so \
274	//!     my_kernel.rs
275	//! ```
276	//!
277	//! ## Environment Variables
278	//!
279	//! | Variable                          | Effect                               |
280	//! |-----------------------------------|--------------------------------------|
281	//! | `CUDA_OXIDE_VERBOSE`              | Print detailed compilation progress  |
282	//! | `CUDA_OXIDE_DUMP_MIR`             | Dump the `dialect-mir` module        |
283	//! | `CUDA_OXIDE_DUMP_LLVM`            | Dump the LLVM dialect module         |
284	//! | `CUDA_OXIDE_PTX_DIR`              | Override PTX output directory        |
285	//! | `CUDA_OXIDE_TARGET`               | Override GPU target (e.g., `sm_90a`) |
286	//! | `CUDA_OXIDE_DEVICE_CODEGEN_CRATE` | Filter device owner crate names      |
287	//!
288	//! ## Module Structure
289	//!
290	//! - [`collector`]: Device function collection via MIR call graph traversal
291	//! - [`device_codegen`]: Bridge to the cuda-oxide pipeline (MIR → PTX)
292	//! - [`layout`]: Unified type layouts for host/device ABI compatibility
293

295	#![allow(unused_imports)]
296	#![allow(dead_code)]

338	/// The CUDA codegen backend.
339	///
340	/// This backend wraps `rustc_codegen_llvm` for host code while adding
341	/// device code compilation via cuda-oxide. It implements the [`CodegenBackend`]
342	/// trait which rustc uses to delegate code generation.
343	///
344	/// ## Delegation Strategy
345	///
346	/// Rather than reimplementing all of LLVM codegen, we:
347	/// 1. Intercept `codegen_crate()` to extract and compile device code
348	/// 2. Delegate ALL other methods to `rustc_codegen_llvm`
349	///
350	/// This means host code gets the full, battle-tested LLVM backend while
351	/// device code goes through our specialized cuda-oxide pipeline.
352	pub struct CudaCodegenBackend {
353	    config: CudaCodegenConfig,
354	    /// The underlying LLVM backend for host code generation
355	    llvm_backend: Box<dyn CodegenBackend>,
356	}
357	
358	struct CudaOngoingCodegen {
359	    host: Box<dyn Any>,
360	    artifact_objects: Vec<PathBuf>,
361	}
362	
363	static ARTIFACT_OBJECT_COUNTER: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0);
364	
365	/// Configuration for the CUDA codegen backend.
366	///
367	/// All configuration is read from environment variables at backend load time.
368	/// This avoids the need to thread configuration through rustc's argument parsing.
369	#[derive(Clone, Default)]
370	pub struct CudaCodegenConfig {
371	    /// Print detailed compilation progress to stderr.
372	    pub verbose: bool,
373	    /// Dump raw rustc MIR before translation (requires --verbose flag).
374	    pub dump_rustc_mir: bool,
375	    /// Dump the `dialect-mir` module during device compilation.
376	    pub dump_mir_dialect: bool,
377	    /// Dump the LLVM dialect module during device compilation.
378	    pub dump_llvm_dialect: bool,
379	    /// Override PTX output directory (defaults to current directory).
380	    pub ptx_output_dir: Option<std::path::PathBuf>,
381	    /// When set, emit device code only for these normalized local crate names.
382	    /// Host code still goes through the wrapped LLVM backend for every crate.
383	    pub device_codegen_crates: Option<BTreeSet<String>>,
384	}
385	
386	impl CudaCodegenConfig {
387	    /// Load configuration from environment variables.
388	    ///
389	    /// | Variable                             | Config Field             |
390	    /// |--------------------------------------|--------------------------|
391	    /// | `CUDA_OXIDE_VERBOSE`                 | `verbose`                |
392	    /// | `CUDA_OXIDE_SHOW_RUSTC_MIR`          | `dump_rustc_mir`         |
393	    /// | `CUDA_OXIDE_DUMP_MIR`                | `dump_mir_dialect`       |
394	    /// | `CUDA_OXIDE_DUMP_LLVM`               | `dump_llvm_dialect`      |
395	    /// | `CUDA_OXIDE_PTX_DIR`                 | `ptx_output_dir`         |
396	    /// | `CUDA_OXIDE_DEVICE_CODEGEN_CRATE`  | `device_codegen_crates`  |
397	    pub fn from_env() -> Self {
398	        Self {
399	            verbose: std::env::var("CUDA_OXIDE_VERBOSE").is_ok(),
400	            dump_rustc_mir: std::env::var("CUDA_OXIDE_SHOW_RUSTC_MIR").is_ok(),
401	            dump_mir_dialect: std::env::var("CUDA_OXIDE_DUMP_MIR").is_ok(),
402	            dump_llvm_dialect: std::env::var("CUDA_OXIDE_DUMP_LLVM").is_ok(),
403	            ptx_output_dir: std::env::var("CUDA_OXIDE_PTX_DIR")
404	                .ok()
405	                .map(std::path::PathBuf::from),
406	            device_codegen_crates: parse_device_codegen_crates(
407	                std::env::var("CUDA_OXIDE_DEVICE_CODEGEN_CRATE")
408	                    .ok()
409	                    .as_deref(),
410	            ),
411	        }
412	    }
413	
414	    fn allows_device_codegen_for(&self, crate_name: &str) -> bool {
415	        self.device_codegen_crates
416	            .as_ref()
417	            .is_none_or(|owners| owners.contains(&normalize_device_crate_name(crate_name)))
418	    }
419	}
420	
421	fn normalize_device_crate_name(name: &str) -> String {
422	    name.trim().replace('-', "_")
423	}
424	
425	fn parse_device_codegen_crates(raw: Option<&str>) -> Option<BTreeSet<String>> {
426	    raw.and_then(|raw| {
427	        let owners: BTreeSet<_> = raw
428	            .split(',')
429	            .map(normalize_device_crate_name)
430	            .filter(|name| !name.is_empty())
431	            .collect();
432	        (!owners.is_empty()).then_some(owners)
433	    })
434	}
435	
436	fn should_codegen_device_crate(
437	    config: &CudaCodegenConfig,
438	    crate_name: &str,
439	    contains_device_code: bool,
440	) -> bool {
441	    contains_device_code && config.allows_device_codegen_for(crate_name)
442	}
443	
444	impl CodegenBackend for CudaCodegenBackend {
445	    fn name(&self) -> &'static str {
446	        "cuda"
447	    }
448	
449	    fn init(&self, sess: &Session) {
450	        // Note: Don't log here - init() is called for ALL crates including dependencies.
451	        // We log in codegen_crate() only when there are kernels to compile.
452	
453	        // Initialize the underlying LLVM backend
454	        self.llvm_backend.init(sess);
455	    }
456	
457	    fn print_version(&self) {
458	        println!(
459	            "rustc_codegen_cuda version {} (wrapping rustc_codegen_llvm)",
460	            env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION")
461	        );
462	        self.llvm_backend.print_version();
463	    }
464	
465	    fn target_cpu(&self, sess: &Session) -> String {
466	        self.llvm_backend.target_cpu(sess)
467	    }
468	
469	    fn target_config(&self, sess: &Session) -> rustc_codegen_ssa::TargetConfig {
470	        self.llvm_backend.target_config(sess)
471	    }
472	
473	    fn provide(&self, providers: &mut rustc_middle::util::Providers) {
474	        // Delegate to LLVM backend
475	        self.llvm_backend.provide(providers);
476	    }
477	
478	    /// Main codegen entry point - this is where device/host splitting happens.
479	    ///
480	    /// ## Execution Flow
481	    ///
482	    /// ```text
483	    /// codegen_crate(TyCtxt)
484	    ///       │
485	    ///       ├──▶ 1. Get monomorphized items from rustc
486	    ///       │       tcx.collect_and_partition_mono_items()
487	    ///       │
488	    ///       ├──▶ 2. Count kernels (functions in the reserved cuda_oxide_kernel_ namespace)
489	    ///       │
490	    ///       ├──▶ 3. If kernels found:
491	    ///       │       │
492	    ///       │       ├──▶ collector::collect_device_functions()
493	    ///       │       │       Walk call graph from kernels
494	    ///       │       │       Return Vec<CollectedFunction>
495	    ///       │       │
496	    ///       │       └──▶ device_codegen::generate_device_code()
497	    ///       │               Enter stable_mir context
498	    ///       │               Convert instances
499	    ///       │               Call mir_importer::run_pipeline()
500	    ///       │               Output: .ll and .ptx files
501	    ///       │
502	    ///       └──▶ 4. llvm_backend.codegen_crate(tcx)
503	    ///               Let LLVM handle ALL host code
504	    /// ```
505	    fn codegen_crate(&self, tcx: TyCtxt<'_>, crate_info: &CrateInfo) -> Box<dyn Any> {
506	        // Wrap entire function in with_no_trimmed_paths! to prevent diagnostic state issues.
507	        // This is necessary because we use tcx.def_path_str() and other functions that
508	        // trigger trimmed_def_paths. rust-gpu uses the same pattern.
509	        with_no_trimmed_paths!({
510	            // Step 1: Analyze for device code
511	            let mono_partitions = tcx.collect_and_partition_mono_items(());
512	            let kernel_count = collector::count_kernels_in_cgus(tcx, mono_partitions.codegen_units);
513	            let device_fn_count =
514	                collector::count_device_fns_in_cgus(tcx, mono_partitions.codegen_units);
515	            let crate_name = tcx.crate_name(rustc_hir::def_id::LOCAL_CRATE);
516	            let owner_selected = self.config.allows_device_codegen_for(crate_name.as_str());
517	            let contains_device_code = kernel_count > 0 || device_fn_count > 0;
518	            let has_device_code = should_codegen_device_crate(
519	                &self.config,
520	                crate_name.as_str(),
521	                contains_device_code,
522	            );
523	            let mut artifact_objects = Vec::new();
524	
525	            if self.config.verbose && contains_device_code && !owner_selected {
526	                eprintln!(
527	                    "[rustc_codegen_cuda] Skipping device code for crate '{}' because it is not in CUDA_OXIDE_DEVICE_CODEGEN_CRATE",
528	                    crate_name
529	                );
530	            }
531	
532	            // Older `#[cuda_module]` expansions always reference the legacy
533	            // package-level anchor. An owner filter deliberately suppresses
534	            // this crate's device artifact, but mixed-version host code must
535	            // still link. Supply a weak legacy anchor-only object without an
536	            // `.oxart` bundle. New owner-aware macros omit the reference for
537	            // unselected crates and do not need the fallback.
538	            if kernel_count > 0 && !owner_selected {
539	                let output_dir = self
540	                    .config
541	                    .ptx_output_dir
542	                    .clone()
543	                    .unwrap_or_else(|| std::env::current_dir().unwrap_or_else(|_| ".".into()));
544	                match write_filtered_artifact_anchor_object(
545	                    &output_dir,
546	                    crate_name.as_str(),
547	                    tcx.sess.target.llvm_target.as_ref(),
548	                ) {
549	                    Ok(path) => artifact_objects.push(path),
550	                    Err(error) => tcx.dcx().fatal(format!(
551	                        "[rustc_codegen_cuda] Failed to write filtered artifact anchor: {error}"
552	                    )),
553	                }
554	            }
555	
556	            // Only log for crates that have device code (reduces noise from dependency crates)
557	            if self.config.verbose && has_device_code {
558	                eprintln!(
559	                    "[rustc_codegen_cuda] Compiling crate '{}': {} CGUs, {} kernel(s), {} device fn(s)",
560	                    crate_name,
561	                    mono_partitions.codegen_units.len(),
562	                    kernel_count,
563	                    device_fn_count
564	                );
565	            }
566	
567	            // Step 2: If device code exists, compile via cuda-oxide
568	            let _device_result = if has_device_code {
569	                if self.config.verbose {
570	                    eprintln!("[rustc_codegen_cuda] Compiling device code via cuda-oxide...");
571	                }
572	
573	                // Collect all device-reachable functions (kernels + their callees)
574	                let collection_result = collector::collect_device_functions(
575	                    tcx,
576	                    mono_partitions.codegen_units,
577	                    self.config.verbose,
578	                );
579	
580	                if self.config.verbose {
581	                    eprintln!(
582	                        "[rustc_codegen_cuda] Collected {} device functions, {} device externs for PTX compilation",
583	                        collection_result.functions.len(),
584	                        collection_result.device_externs.len()
585	                    );
586	
587	                    // Dump MIR info for verification
588	                    collector::dump_device_mir_info(tcx, &collection_result.functions);
589	                }
590	
591	                // Extract references for the pipeline
592	                let device_functions = &collection_result.functions;
593	
594	                // Create device codegen config from our config
595	                let device_config =
596	                    device_codegen::DeviceCodegenConfig {
597	                        output_dir: self.config.ptx_output_dir.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| {
598	                            std::env::current_dir().unwrap_or_else(|_| ".".into())
599	                        }),
600	                        output_name: tcx.crate_name(rustc_hir::def_id::LOCAL_CRATE).to_string(),
601	                        verbose: self.config.verbose,
602	                        dump_rustc_mir: self.config.dump_rustc_mir,
603	                        dump_mir_dialect: self.config.dump_mir_dialect,
604	                        dump_llvm_dialect: self.config.dump_llvm_dialect,
605	                    };
606	
607	                // Run the cuda-oxide pipeline, catching backend panics and
608	                // re-emitting them as a cuda-oxide diagnostic. A panic
609	                // inside the pipeline (typically pliron's IR invariant
610	                // checks) would otherwise escape to rustc's panic hook and
611	                // get dressed up as "the compiler unexpectedly panicked,
612	                // please file a rustc bug". The bug is in cuda-oxide, so
613	                // we want users pointed at our tracker, not rustc's.
614	                //
615	                // We also briefly swap rustc's ICE hook for our own, because
616	                // panic hooks fire *before* catch_unwind catches the unwind.
617	                // Without the swap, the rustc-flavoured banner would still
618	                // print to stderr ahead of our diagnostic. The replacement
619	                // hook also captures a backtrace, since by the time we
620	                // catch the unwind the stack we want is gone. Capture
621	                // honours `RUST_BACKTRACE` so an unset env var still costs
622	                // nothing. Hooks are global; rustc's codegen at this entry
623	                // point is effectively single-threaded, so the brief
624	                // window where the hook is swapped is safe.
625	                let (panic_outcome, panic_backtrace) = {
626	                    use std::backtrace::Backtrace;
627	                    use std::panic::{AssertUnwindSafe, catch_unwind};
628	                    use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};
629	                    let bt_slot: Arc<Mutex<Option<Backtrace>>> = Arc::new(Mutex::new(None));
630	                    let bt_setter = Arc::clone(&bt_slot);
631	                    let prev_hook = std::panic::take_hook();
632	                    std::panic::set_hook(Box::new(move |_info| {
633	                        if let Ok(mut g) = bt_setter.lock() {
634	                            *g = Some(Backtrace::capture());
635	                        }
636	                    }));
637	                    let r = catch_unwind(AssertUnwindSafe(|| {
638	                        device_codegen::generate_device_code(
639	                            tcx,
640	                            device_functions,
641	                            &collection_result.device_externs,
642	                            &device_config,
643	                        )
644	                    }));
645	                    std::panic::set_hook(prev_hook);
646	                    (r, bt_slot)
647	                };
648	
649	                match panic_outcome {
650	                    Err(payload) => {
651	                        let msg = payload
652	                            .downcast_ref::<&str>()
653	                            .map(|s| s.to_string())
654	                            .or_else(|| payload.downcast_ref::<String>().cloned())
655	                            .unwrap_or_else(|| "<opaque panic payload>".into());
656	                        match panic_backtrace.lock().ok().and_then(|mut g| g.take()) {
657	                            Some(bt)
658	                                if bt.status() == std::backtrace::BacktraceStatus::Captured =>
659	                            {
660	                                eprintln!("[rustc_codegen_cuda] backtrace:\n{bt}");
661	                            }
662	                            _ => {
663	                                eprintln!(
664	                                    "[rustc_codegen_cuda] note: run with `RUST_BACKTRACE=1` to display a backtrace"
665	                                );
666	                            }
667	                        }
668	                        tcx.dcx().fatal(format!(
669	                            "[rustc_codegen_cuda] Internal compiler error in \
670	                             device codegen: {msg}. This is a bug in cuda-oxide. \
671	                             Please file at https://github.com/NVlabs/cuda-oxide/issues"
672	                        ));
673	                    }
674	                    Ok(Ok(result)) => {
675	                        if self.config.verbose
676	                            && let Some(artifact) = result.artifact.as_ref()
677	                        {
678	                            eprintln!(
679	                                "[rustc_codegen_cuda] Device codegen complete: {} ({:?}, target: {})",
680	                                artifact.name, artifact.kind, result.target
681	                            );
682	                        }
683	                        if let Some(artifact) = result.artifact.as_ref() {
684	                            match write_device_artifact_object(
685	                                &device_config.output_dir,
686	                                &device_config.output_name,
687	                                tcx.sess.target.llvm_target.as_ref(),
688	                                &result,
689	                                artifact,
690	                                device_functions,
691	                                self.config.device_codegen_crates.is_some(),
692	                            ) {
693	                                Ok(path) => {
694	                                    if self.config.verbose {
695	                                        eprintln!(
696	                                            "[rustc_codegen_cuda] Embedded artifact object complete: {}",
697	                                            path.display()
698	                                        );
699	                                    }
700	                                    artifact_objects.push(path);
701	                                }
702	                                Err(e) => {
703	                                    tcx.dcx().fatal(format!(
704	                                        "[rustc_codegen_cuda] Failed to embed device artifact: {e}"
705	                                    ));
706	                                }
707	                            }
708	                        } else {
709	                            tcx.dcx().fatal(
710	                                "[rustc_codegen_cuda] Device codegen did not produce an embeddable artifact",
711	                            );
712	                        }
713	                        Some(result)
714	                    }
715	                    Ok(Err(e)) => {
716	                        // Hard-fail: a swallowed device codegen error produces
717	                        // a host binary with stale or missing PTX, which then
718	                        // silently mis-runs on the GPU. The wrapper script
719	                        // (cargo-oxide) reports "✓ Build succeeded" in that
720	                        // case because the host LLVM backend below succeeds.
721	                        // Surface the failure as a rustc fatal so cargo exits
722	                        // non-zero and the wrapper's success print never fires.
723	                        // See `.cursor/rules/compiler-gaps-are-bugs.mdc`.
724	                        tcx.dcx()
725	                            .fatal(format!("[rustc_codegen_cuda] Device codegen failed: {}", e));
726	                    }
727	                }
728	            } else {
729	                None
730	            };
731	
732	            // Step 3: Delegate ALL host codegen to LLVM backend
733	            // (No logging here - it fires for every crate including dependencies)
734	            let host_result = self.llvm_backend.codegen_crate(tcx, crate_info);
735	
736	            // Return the LLVM backend's result
737	            Box::new(CudaOngoingCodegen {
738	                host: host_result,
739	                artifact_objects,
740	            })
741	        })
742	    }
743	
744	    fn join_codegen(
745	        &self,
746	        ongoing_codegen: Box<dyn Any>,
747	        sess: &Session,
748	        outputs: &OutputFilenames,
749	    ) -> (CompiledModules, FxIndexMap<WorkProductId, WorkProduct>) {
750	        let ongoing = *ongoing_codegen
751	            .downcast::<CudaOngoingCodegen>()
752	            .expect("rustc_codegen_cuda received unexpected ongoing codegen state");
753	        let (mut compiled_modules, work_products) =
754	            self.llvm_backend.join_codegen(ongoing.host, sess, outputs);
755	        for (index, object) in ongoing.artifact_objects.into_iter().enumerate() {
756	            compiled_modules.modules.push(CompiledModule {
757	                name: format!("oxide_artifact_embed_{index}"),
758	                kind: ModuleKind::Regular,
759	                object: Some(object),
760	                dwarf_object: None,
761	                bytecode: None,
762	                assembly: None,
763	                llvm_ir: None,
764	                links_from_incr_cache: Vec::new(),
765	            });
766	        }
767	        (compiled_modules, work_products)
768	    }
769	
770	    fn link(
771	        &self,
772	        sess: &Session,
773	        compiled_modules: CompiledModules,
774	        crate_info: CrateInfo,
775	        metadata: EncodedMetadata,
776	        outputs: &OutputFilenames,
777	    ) {
778	        self.llvm_backend
779	            .link(sess, compiled_modules, crate_info, metadata, outputs);
780	    }
781	}
782	
783	fn write_device_artifact_object(
784	    output_dir: &Path,
785	    output_name: &str,
786	    host_target: &str,
787	    result: &device_codegen::DeviceCodegenResult,
788	    artifact: &device_codegen::DeviceCodegenArtifact,
789	    functions: &[collector::CollectedFunction<'_>],
790	    use_target_specific_anchor: bool,
791	) -> Result<PathBuf, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
792	    let bundle_name = std::env::var("CARGO_PKG_NAME").unwrap_or_else(|_| output_name.to_string());
793	    let payload_kind = match artifact.kind {
794	        device_codegen::DeviceCodegenArtifactKind::Ptx => oxide_artifacts::ArtifactPayloadKind::Ptx,
795	        device_codegen::DeviceCodegenArtifactKind::NvvmIr => {
796	            oxide_artifacts::ArtifactPayloadKind::NvvmIr
797	        }
798	        device_codegen::DeviceCodegenArtifactKind::Ltoir => {
799	            oxide_artifacts::ArtifactPayloadKind::Ltoir
800	        }
801	        device_codegen::DeviceCodegenArtifactKind::Cubin => {
802	            oxide_artifacts::ArtifactPayloadKind::Cubin
803	        }
804	    };
805	    let compile_options = if matches!(
806	        artifact.kind,
807	        device_codegen::DeviceCodegenArtifactKind::NvvmIr
808	            | device_codegen::DeviceCodegenArtifactKind::Ltoir
809	    ) {
810	        oxide_artifacts::ArtifactCompileOptions::new()
811	            .with_fma_contraction(result.allow_fma_contraction)
812	    } else {
813	        oxide_artifacts::ArtifactCompileOptions::new()
814	    };
815	    let mut spec = oxide_artifacts::ArtifactBundleSpec::new(&bundle_name, &result.target)
816	        .with_compile_options(compile_options)
817	        .with_payload(oxide_artifacts::ArtifactPayloadSpec::new(
818	            payload_kind,
819	            &artifact.name,
820	            &artifact.bytes,
821	        ));
822	    for function in functions {
823	        let kind = if function.is_kernel {
824	            oxide_artifacts::ArtifactEntryKind::Kernel
825	        } else {
826	            oxide_artifacts::ArtifactEntryKind::DeviceFunction
827	        };
828	        spec = spec.with_entry(oxide_artifacts::ArtifactEntrySpec::new(
829	            &function.export_name,
830	            kind,
831	        ));
832	    }
833	
834	    let blob = oxide_artifacts::build_artifact_blob(&spec)?;
835	    // Define a link-anchor symbol at the start of the `.oxart` data. When
836	    // this crate is a library, the artifact object becomes an rlib archive
837	    // member, and the linker only extracts it if some other object holds an
838	    // undefined reference to a symbol defined here. The `#[cuda_module]`
839	    // macro emits that reference from the generated `load_named()`. Normal
840	    // builds preserve the legacy package-level symbol. Owner-filtered builds
841	    // use a target-specific v2 symbol plus a weak legacy alias, so an older
842	    // macro still links without letting a filtered target hide a selected
843	    // target's artifact. Without an anchor, library-crate bundles were
844	    // dead-stripped and `load()` failed at runtime with ModuleNotFound
845	    // (issue #72).
846	    let package_version = std::env::var("CARGO_PKG_VERSION").unwrap_or_default();
847	    let legacy_anchor =
848	        reserved_oxide_symbols::artifact_anchor_symbol(&bundle_name, &package_version);
849	    let object = if use_target_specific_anchor {
850	        let binary_name = std::env::var("CARGO_BIN_NAME").ok();
851	        let target_anchor = reserved_oxide_symbols::artifact_anchor_symbol_v2(
852	            &bundle_name,
853	            &package_version,
854	            output_name,
855	            binary_name.as_deref(),
856	        );
857	        oxide_artifacts::build_host_object_for_target_with_legacy_anchor(
858	            &blob,
859	            host_target,
860	            &target_anchor,
861	            &legacy_anchor,
862	        )?
863	    } else {
864	        oxide_artifacts::build_host_object_for_target(&blob, host_target, Some(&legacy_anchor))?
865	    };
866	    write_artifact_object(output_dir, output_name, host_target, &object, "embed")
867	}
868	
869	fn write_filtered_artifact_anchor_object(
870	    output_dir: &Path,
871	    output_name: &str,
872	    host_target: &str,
873	) -> Result<PathBuf, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
874	    let bundle_name = std::env::var("CARGO_PKG_NAME").unwrap_or_else(|_| output_name.to_string());
875	    let package_version = std::env::var("CARGO_PKG_VERSION").unwrap_or_default();
876	    let anchor_symbol =
877	        reserved_oxide_symbols::artifact_anchor_symbol(&bundle_name, &package_version);
878	    let object = oxide_artifacts::build_host_anchor_object_for_target(host_target, &anchor_symbol)?;
879	    write_artifact_object(output_dir, output_name, host_target, &object, "anchor")
880	}
881	
882	fn write_artifact_object(
883	    output_dir: &Path,
884	    output_name: &str,
885	    host_target: &str,
886	    object: &[u8],
887	    kind: &str,
888	) -> Result<PathBuf, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
889	    let safe_output_name = sanitize_path_component(output_name);
890	    let artifact_id = ARTIFACT_OBJECT_COUNTER.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
891	    let object_dir = output_dir
892	        .join(".oxide-artifacts")
893	        .join(&safe_output_name)
894	        .join(sanitize_path_component(host_target));
895	    std::fs::create_dir_all(&object_dir)?;
896	    let object_path = object_dir.join(format!(
897	        "{safe_output_name}.{}.{artifact_id}.{kind}.o",
898	        std::process::id(),
899	    ));
900	    std::fs::write(&object_path, object)?;
901	    Ok(object_path)
902	}
903	
904	fn sanitize_path_component(name: &str) -> String {
905	    name.chars()
906	        .map(|ch| match ch {
907	            'a'..='z' | 'A'..='Z' | '0'..='9' | '-' | '_' | '.' => ch,
908	            _ => '_',
909	        })
910	        .collect()
911	}
912	
913	/// Entry point called by rustc to instantiate the backend.
914	///
915	/// This function is discovered by rustc via the `#[no_mangle]` attribute and the
916	/// specific name `__rustc_codegen_backend`. When a user specifies
917	/// `-Z codegen-backend=path/to/librustc_codegen_cuda.so`, rustc loads the shared
918	/// library and calls this function to get a `Box<dyn CodegenBackend>`.
919	///
920	/// ## Initialization Sequence
921	///
922	/// ```text
923	/// rustc -Z codegen-backend=librustc_codegen_cuda.so ...
924	///       │
925	///       ├──▶ dlopen("librustc_codegen_cuda.so")
926	///       │
927	///       ├──▶ dlsym("__rustc_codegen_backend")
928	///       │
929	///       └──▶ __rustc_codegen_backend()
930	///               │
931	///               ├──▶ CudaCodegenConfig::from_env()
932	///               │       Read CUDA_OXIDE_* env vars
933	///               │
934	///               ├──▶ rustc_codegen_llvm::LlvmCodegenBackend::new()
935	///               │       Create the wrapped LLVM backend
936	///               │
937	///               └──▶ Return Box<CudaCodegenBackend>
938	/// ```
939	#[unsafe(no_mangle)]
940	pub fn __rustc_codegen_backend() -> Box<dyn CodegenBackend> {
941	    let config = CudaCodegenConfig::from_env();
942	
943	    // Note: Don't log here - this function is called for EVERY crate in the dependency tree.
944	    // We log in codegen_crate() only when there are kernels to compile.
945	
946	    // Get the LLVM backend - this is the same function rustc calls normally
947	    let llvm_backend = rustc_codegen_llvm::LlvmCodegenBackend::new();
948	
949	    Box::new(CudaCodegenBackend {
950	        config,
951	        llvm_backend,
952	    })
953	}
954	
955	#[cfg(test)]
956	mod tests {
957	    use super::*;
958	
959	    #[test]
960	    fn device_codegen_owner_filter_normalizes_and_matches_crate_names() {
961	        let owners = parse_device_codegen_crates(Some(" gpu-kernels,math_gpu , gpu-kernels "))
962	            .expect("an explicitly configured filter");
963	        assert_eq!(
964	            owners,
965	            BTreeSet::from(["gpu_kernels".to_string(), "math_gpu".to_string()])
966	        );
967	
968	        let config = CudaCodegenConfig {
969	            device_codegen_crates: Some(owners),
970	            ..CudaCodegenConfig::default()
971	        };
972	        assert!(config.allows_device_codegen_for("gpu_kernels"));
973	        assert!(config.allows_device_codegen_for("gpu-kernels"));
974	        assert!(config.allows_device_codegen_for("math_gpu"));
975	        assert!(!config.allows_device_codegen_for("host_app"));
976	        assert!(should_codegen_device_crate(&config, "gpu_kernels", true));
977	        assert!(!should_codegen_device_crate(&config, "host_app", true));
978	        assert!(!should_codegen_device_crate(&config, "gpu_kernels", false));
979	    }
980	
981	    #[test]
982	    fn absent_owner_filter_allows_device_codegen_for_every_crate() {
983	        let config = CudaCodegenConfig::default();
984	        assert!(config.allows_device_codegen_for("gpu_kernels"));
985	        assert!(config.allows_device_codegen_for("host_app"));
986	    }
987	
988	    #[test]
989	    fn empty_owner_filter_is_treated_as_unset() {
990	        let config = CudaCodegenConfig {
991	            device_codegen_crates: parse_device_codegen_crates(Some(" , ")),
992	            ..CudaCodegenConfig::default()
993	        };
994	        assert!(config.allows_device_codegen_for("gpu_kernels"));
995	    }
996	}
